questions.q: What does the acronym GSLV stand for?
questions.explanation: GSLV stands for Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle, designed to place satellites into geosynchronous transfer orbit.

questions.q: Which Indian organisation develops and operates the GSLV?
questions.explanation: The GSLV is developed and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: What type of engine technology is the GSLV best known for mastering?
questions.explanation: The GSLV is famous for mastering the difficult indigenous cryogenic engine, used in its upper stage.

questions.q: What kind of orbit is the GSLV primarily designed to deliver satellites to?
questions.explanation: The GSLV is primarily designed to place communication satellites into geosynchronous transfer orbit (GTO).

questions.q: Cryogenic engines use propellants that are stored at which temperature condition?
questions.explanation: Cryogenic engines use propellants such as liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen stored at extremely low temperatures.

questions.q: From which spaceport are GSLV rockets launched?
questions.explanation: GSLV rockets are launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota, ISRO's main spaceport.

questions.q: The GSLV was developed mainly to launch which category of Indian satellites?
questions.explanation: The GSLV was developed mainly to launch India's heavy INSAT/GSAT communication satellites into geosynchronous orbit.

questions.q: Which ISRO rocket is often nicknamed the 'workhorse' that complements the heavier-lift GSLV?
questions.explanation: The PSLV is known as ISRO's reliable 'workhorse', while the GSLV handles heavier geosynchronous launches.

questions.q: What does the word 'indigenous' emphasise about ISRO's GSLV cryogenic engine?
options: It was purchased ready-made from the United States
It was developed within India by Indian scientists
questions.explanation: Indigenous means the cryogenic engine was designed and developed within India by ISRO scientists rather than imported.

questions.q: Cryogenic upper-stage engines are valued because, compared to other chemical engines, they generally offer higher what?
questions.explanation: Cryogenic engines provide higher specific impulse (greater fuel efficiency), which is why they are prized for upper stages.

questions.q: Which pair of propellants is used in the GSLV's cryogenic upper stage?
questions.explanation: The GSLV cryogenic upper stage burns liquid hydrogen (fuel) with liquid oxygen (oxidiser).

questions.q: In which year did the GSLV make its first developmental flight (GSLV-D1)?
questions.explanation: The GSLV's first developmental flight, GSLV-D1, took place in April 2001.

questions.q: Early GSLV flights used a cryogenic upper stage sourced from which country before India's own engine flew?
questions.explanation: Early GSLV flights used Russian-supplied cryogenic stages while ISRO developed its own indigenous engine.

questions.q: What is the name of India's indigenous cryogenic upper-stage engine used on the GSLV Mk II?
questions.explanation: The GSLV Mk II uses ISRO's indigenous CE-7.5 cryogenic upper-stage engine.

questions.q: In January 2014, ISRO achieved a major milestone when which flight first successfully used the indigenous cryogenic stage?
questions.explanation: GSLV-D5, launched in January 2014, was the first fully successful flight using India's indigenous cryogenic upper stage.

questions.q: Roughly how many propulsion stages does the GSLV Mk II have?
questions.explanation: The GSLV Mk II is a three-stage rocket: a solid first stage, a liquid second stage, and a cryogenic upper stage.

questions.q: The liquid-fuelled engine powering the GSLV's second stage and strap-on boosters is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The GSLV's lower liquid stages use the Vikas engine, developed by ISRO from the licensed French Viking engine technology.

questions.q: Why are cryogenic engines considered technically very difficult to build?
options: They require handling and pumping propellants at extremely low temperatures
They cannot produce any thrust in vacuum
questions.explanation: Cryogenic engines are hard because they must store, pump and combust propellants kept at extremely low temperatures.

questions.q: Which class of Indian satellites has frequently been launched by the GSLV?
questions.explanation: The GSLV has launched several GSAT-series communication satellites into geosynchronous transfer orbit.

questions.q: Approximately what payload mass can the GSLV Mk II place into geosynchronous transfer orbit?
questions.explanation: The GSLV Mk II can place roughly 2,000–2,500 kg into geosynchronous transfer orbit.

questions.q: The GSLV's first stage uses what type of propellant motor?
questions.explanation: The GSLV's core first stage is a large solid-propellant motor, with liquid strap-on boosters around it.

questions.q: Mastering the cryogenic engine was strategically important for India largely because of what?
options: Technology-denial restrictions had limited foreign cryogenic supply
Cryogenic engines are cheaper than paper
India had banned all rocket launches
No other country had cryogenic engines
questions.explanation: Foreign technology-denial pressures pushed India to develop its own cryogenic engine for self-reliance.

questions.q: The heavy-lift variant developed in the GSLV programme line, used for Chandrayaan-2 and Gaganyaan, is now formally designated as what?
questions.explanation: The heavy-lift GSLV Mk III has been redesignated LVM3 and was used for Chandrayaan-2 and is planned for Gaganyaan.

questions.q: What is the name of the cryogenic engine that powers the upper stage of the LVM3 (GSLV Mk III)?
questions.explanation: The LVM3 (GSLV Mk III) uses the higher-thrust indigenous CE-20 cryogenic upper-stage engine, distinct from the Mk II's CE-7.5.

questions.q: The large solid strap-on boosters flanking the LVM3 (GSLV Mk III) core are designated as what?
questions.explanation: The LVM3 uses two large S200 solid boosters; L110 is its liquid core and C25 its cryogenic upper stage.

questions.q: In ISRO's stage nomenclature, the 'C25' designation on the GSLV Mk III/LVM3 refers to what?
questions.explanation: C25 denotes a cryogenic ('C') stage carrying about 25 tonnes of propellant on the LVM3.

questions.q: The GSLV-F10 mission in August 2021, intended to launch the EOS-03 satellite, ended how?
options: A failure due to a cryogenic upper-stage anomaly
questions.explanation: The GSLV-F10 (EOS-03) flight failed in August 2021 after a performance anomaly in the cryogenic upper stage.

questions.q: Before reliable Indian cryogenic flights, the GSLV programme suffered notable setbacks; what happened on the April 2010 GSLV-D3 flight, the first to fly India's indigenous cryogenic stage?
options: It placed a satellite in orbit flawlessly
The indigenous cryogenic stage failed and the mission did not succeed
questions.explanation: GSLV-D3 in April 2010 was the maiden flight of India's indigenous cryogenic stage, but it malfunctioned and the mission failed.

questions.q: Which interplanetary or lunar mission was launched on the heavy-lift GSLV Mk III (LVM3) line in 2019?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-2 was launched in July 2019 aboard the heavy-lift GSLV Mk III (LVM3).

questions.q: Compared with the GSLV Mk II, the GSLV Mk III (LVM3) offered roughly what improvement in geosynchronous transfer orbit payload capacity?
options: Roughly double, to around 4,000 kg to GTO
questions.explanation: The GSLV Mk III (LVM3) can carry about 4,000 kg to GTO, roughly double the GSLV Mk II's capacity.
